commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8d8cb554d1b8e6a5d56da40db860761ec49bd696)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds cooperative event-loop yields to the Ollama NDJSON stream parser after dense record bursts and adds a regression test that timers fire mid-burst.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +29. Total +50 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, though I did not run tests in this read-only review. Current main loops over every parsed same-read Ollama NDJSON record without a macrotask yield, and the PR adds a 128-record regression that checks timer progress by record 65.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair is needed because the patch is focused and I found no actionable code defect; remaining action is maintainer readiness and review, not an automated fix.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense ndjson bursts
target.body:
## Summary
- add cooperative event-loop yields while parsing large Ollama NDJSON bursts from one read
- mirror the existing OpenAI stream behavior with a 64-record / 12ms threshold
- add regression coverage proving timers can run mid-burst instead of waiting for the whole payload to parse

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts` - 1 file / 99 tests passed on `8d8cb554`
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `./.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean
- Crabbox AWS Linux changed gate: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --timing-json --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"`, run `run_08008110f420`, lease `cbx_aa144d583928`, slug `quick-prawn`, exit 0. `origin/main` moved afterward; branch was cleanly rebased and focused sanity checks above were rerun on `8d8cb554`.

Behavior addressed: Ollama provider parsing no longer monopolizes the Gateway event loop when a single read contains many NDJSON records.

Real environment tested: focused local Ollama runtime tests/lint and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: regression test schedules a timer before parsing 128 same-read records and asserts the timer has fired by record 65.

Observed result after fix: dense Ollama stream bursts yield to timers during parsing, reducing Gateway timer/RPC starvation risk.

What was not tested: full live Windows/Ollama UI reproduction; this is the provider dense-loop slice only.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
